always.why: Baseline; screens antalgic posture, torticollis, forward-head position, muscle guarding.
Establishes willingness, range and directional provocation (extension/rotation for facet).
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens restriction pattern and directional provocation

always.why: Differentiates contractile vs articular restriction and localises segmental stiffness.

add.exam: Vascular / VBI + upper cervical ligament screen (IFOMPT framework) FIRST
add.why: Dizziness/vertebrobasilar symptoms must be cleared before any end-range or manual testing — safety-critical.
add.confirmsOrExcludes: Excludes vascular / upper-cervical instability before provocation

add.exam: Neurological screen (dermatomes, myotomes, reflexes) + Wainner cluster (Spurling, distraction, ULTT-A, rotation <60°)
add.why: Radiating arm pain requires excluding/confirming cervical radiculopathy; the Wainner cluster of 3-4 positives materially raises probability.

add.exam: Upper motor neuron screen (Hoffmann, hyperreflexia, clonus, gait) — urgent referral if positive
add.why: Gait disturbance raises myelopathy concern; UMN signs mandate urgent referral, not local treatment.
Neck-related headache warrants testing the upper cervical segments; reduced flexion-rotation supports cervicogenic headache.

add.exam: Canadian C-Spine Rule screen + ROM tolerance
add.why: Traumatic onset requires clearing fracture risk (Canadian C-Spine Rule) before loaded testing.
Extension-rotation provocation localises facet (zygapophyseal) joint pain.

supporting.why: Localises symptomatic segment and tender structures.
Quantifies baseline disability for goal-setting and re-assessment.

supporting.exam: Imaging (only if red flag, trauma failing C-Spine Rule, or progressive neuro deficit)
supporting.why: Imaging is not routine for mechanical neck pain; reserve for red flags and progressive deficit.
